description: Simulate a more complex backend store server that would be developed separately.
Support a hierarchical value-tree store addressed by logical keys.
Allow a value node to carry free text, numbers, booleans, dates, and similar higher-level formats while remaining a protocol-level string.
Reject writes when the submitted version is not based on the latest stored version.
Allow clients to bootstrap local state from an authoritative snapshot for a root key.
Allow clients to stay in sync through incremental events after the snapshot baseline.
Allow clients to keep the same logical data in a local key-value store for sync and offline-friendly flows.
Allow clients to use provisional localKeyId values until the server returns the official keyId.
Derive kind from keyId on the server so filtering and validation use the authoritative key schema.
Allow different value nodes in the same document to use different ISO language codes.
Require secureKeyId as an integrity check so corrupted or forged key identities can be detected.
Allow the mock server to force protocol status responses through test-oriented configuration hooks.
Be sufficient to test client code against a realistic mock server.
Keep WebSocket support optional because some clients may not support it.
Limit WebSocket messages to well-known identifiers to reduce escaping and safety concerns.
Use heartbeat ping/pong messages to keep WebSocket connections alive.
